ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Continued To Oct-01-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Shea for and with Tony Yao and for John Yoa (Co-Administrators).

Mr. Shea stated that this case is moving very slow, that their main focus is to get the tax returns done, that the tax information is in the possession of the CPA, that the CPA is working on it and that he is hopeful that the petition for final distribution can be done within 60-days. Status on close of estate is continued to October 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed one week before the hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Shea for and with John Yao and for Tony Yao (Co-Administrators).

Mr. Shea stated that the petition was improvidently filed, asked the Court to defer the matter and stated that he will file a new petition. Mr. Shea also stated that the tax returns need to be filed. The First and Final Report of Co-Administrators is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for February 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Shea and Georgiann Rovai for and with Robert Yao. John Yao and Tony Yao (Nominees) also present. Agnes Yao in Propria Persona.

Mr. Shea stated that there is no indication that there is a will of any kind or no nominees of any kind, that they are in the same position and asked the Court to grant the petition. Ms. Yao explained circumstances, stated that she used her own personal funds to maintain the properties/paid expenses of the properties, spoke of conversations she had with her Siblings with regard to a plan to purchase/split the properties, stated that she objects to the petition, etc. The Parties confirmed that there are three properties (Bush Street/Cleary Court/California Street). The Court explained that it is time for the Estate to be managed according to the law, stated that it will leave it up to the Parties to determine how to split the properties and that it will make sure the properties are properly distributed to the correct Heirs if they are unable to work things out. The Court overruled Ms. Yao's objections and granted the Petition for Letters of Administration appointing John Yao and Tony Yao as Administrators (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $1,800,000.00 is required. The Court also encouraged Ms. Yao to cooperate with the administrators and turn over bank statements. The Ex Parte Petition for Letters of Special Administration on calendar for December 27, 2013 is off calendar at Counsel's request.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Henry. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Dec-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Georgiann Rovai for and with Robert Yao. Agnes Yao in Propria Persona. Tony Yao in Propria Persona.

This was a pregranted matter. Ms. Yao requested additional time to file her objections. Ms. Rovai stated that she was no notice that Ms. Yao was appearing and asked the Court to adopt the tentative ruling. Ms. Rovai also stated that Ms. Yao is living in the Decedent's property, that there are arrears in the mortgage, that some of the mortgage payments were made by Tony and Robert, and that there are storage lockers that going into auction. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to December 24, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton. (Not Reported)
